html:
2026-0561
2026-06-05JUS, PSCriminal CodeOrder Amending Certain Orders Declaring an Amnesty PeriodOrder Amending Certain Orders Declaring an Amnesty Period in order to (1) extend the validity period of the amnesty orders and set its expiration date at 90 days after the Supreme Court of Canada’s decision is released to provide affected owners with a limited period to come into compliance with the law, should the prohibition be upheld; and (2) allow the affected individuals to ship their prohibited items to a licensed carrier for destruction.Registration: SOR/2026-0114 Publication Date: 2026-06-17
subject: Order Amending Certain Orders Declaring an Amnesty Period
precis: Order Amending Certain Orders Declaring an Amnesty Period in order to (1) extend the validity period of the amnesty orders and set its expiration date at 90 days after the Supreme Court of Canada’s decision is released to provide affected owners with a limited period to come into compliance with the law, should the prohibition be upheld; and (2) allow the affected individuals to ship their prohibited items to a licensed carrier for destruction.
